messages.content: A morphing shape that cycles through rounded corners—starting as sharp squares, progressively bulging into pills, then pinching back to sharp—while its drop shadow intensifies and softens in sync with each border-radius phase, creating a breathing pulse effect.

Five staggered vertical bars that sequentially stroke-dash along their SVG paths while their stroke color shifts from cool blue through purple to hot pink, with each bar's color transition offset by 200ms to create a rolling rainbow wave.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <line class="bar bar1" x1="30" y1="50" x2="30" y2="250" stroke-width="8"/>
  <line class="bar bar2" x1="90" y1="50" x2="90" y2="250" stroke-width="8"/>
  <line class="bar bar3" x1="150" y1="50" x2="150" y2="250" stroke-width="8"/>
  <line class="bar bar4" x1="210" y1="50" x2="210" y2="250" stroke-width="8"/>
  <line class="bar bar5" x1="270" y1="50" x2="270" y2="250" stroke-width="8"/>
</svg>
<style>
.bar {
  stroke-dasharray: 200;
  stroke-dashoffset: 200;
  animation: dashWave 2s ease-in-out infinite;
  stroke-linecap: round;
}

.bar1 {
  animation-delay: 0ms;
}
.bar2 {
  animation-delay: 200ms;
}
.bar3 {
  animation-delay: 400ms;
}
.bar4 {
  animation-delay: 600ms;
}
.bar5 {
  animation-delay: 800ms;
}

@keyframes dashWave {
  0% {
    stroke: #0066cc;
    stroke-dashoffset: 200;
  }
  33% {
    stroke: #9933ff;
    stroke-dashoffset: 100;
  }
  66% {
    stroke: #ff0099;
    stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  }
  100% {
    stroke: #0066cc;
    stroke-dashoffset: 200;
  }
}
</style>

A clipped circular mask that shrinks and expands concentrically while rotating, revealing and concealing layers of a background gradient that shifts position beneath it, synchronized so the clip-path timing creates a pulsing spotlight effect.

Three nested boxes that skew on the X-axis in opposite directions while their sizes grow and shrink inversely—the outer box expands as the inner shrinks—and their shadows cast at different angles to exaggerate the perspective distortion across four sequential phases.

Four concentric circles where the outermost skews on alternating axes while the inner circles counter-skew, their strokes dash-animate in opposite directions at different speeds, and each circle's size oscillates inversely—large strokes thin as inner circles expand—creating a twisting, breathing vortex with staggered timing between each ring.

A particle rain effect where drops translate downward at varying speeds while their opacity fades in and out, individual drops' border-radius shifts from sharp to fully rounded mid-fall, shadows stretch and compress vertically beneath them, and color transitions from cool cyan through warm amber, with each drop's timing offset to create a cascading, flickering waterfall of staggered motion phases.

A radial burst where six geometric petals scale outward from center while simultaneously rotating clockwise, each petal's stroke width pulsing (size), stroke color shifting through a gradient spectrum, and a central shadow expanding then contracting to create concentric rings of depth that accelerate then decelerate across the animation.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <filter id="shadow">
      <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="0" stdDeviation="8" flood-color="#6600ff" flood-opacity="0.7"/>
    </filter>
    <radialGradient id="centerGlow" cx="50%" cy="50%" r="50%">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#ffffff"/>
      <stop offset="60%" stop-color="#aa44ff"/>
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="transparent"/>
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>
  <g class="burst" transform="translate(150,150)">
    <ellipse class="petal p1" rx="14" ry="52" transform="rotate(0)"/>
    <ellipse class="petal p2" rx="14" ry="52" transform="rotate(60)"/>
    <ellipse class="petal p3" rx="14" ry="52" transform="rotate(120)"/>
    <ellipse class="petal p4" rx="14" ry="52" transform="rotate(180)"/>
    <ellipse class="petal p5" rx="14" ry="52" transform="rotate(240)"/>
    <ellipse class="petal p6" rx="14" ry="52" transform="rotate(300)"/>
    <circle class="ring r1" r="30" fill="none"/>
    <circle class="ring r2" r="55" fill="none"/>
    <circle class="ring r3" r="80" fill="none"/>
    <circle class="core" r="18" fill="url(#centerGlow)"/>
  </g>
</svg>
<style>
.burst {
  animation: burstRotate 4s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) infinite;
}
@keyframes burstRotate {
  0%   { transform: translate(150px,150px) rotate(0deg) scale(0.5); }
  30%  { transform: translate(150px,150px) rotate(110deg) scale(1.15); }
  60%  { transform: translate(150px,150px) rotate(230deg) scale(0.9); }
  80%  { transform: translate(150px,150px) rotate(310deg) scale(1.05); }
  100% { transform: translate(150px,150px) rotate(360deg) scale(0.5); }
}
.petal {
  fill: none;
  animation: petalPulse 4s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) infinite;
}
.p1 { animation-delay: 0s; }
.p2 { animation-delay: 0.12s; }
.p3 { animation-delay: 0.24s; }
.p4 { animation-delay: 0.36s; }
.p5 { animation-delay: 0.48s; }
.p6 { animation-delay: 0.60s; }
@keyframes petalPulse {
  0%   { stroke: #ff2266; stroke-width: 2; transform: scaleY(0.4); opacity:0.6; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 2px #ff2266); }
  15%  { stroke: #ff9900; stroke-width: 5; transform: scaleY(0.9); opacity:1;   filter: drop-shadow(0 0 8px #ff9900); }
  30%  { stroke: #ffee00; stroke-width: 8; transform: scaleY(1.2); opacity:1;   filter: drop-shadow(0 0 14px #ffee00); }
  50%  { stroke: #00ffcc; stroke-width: 6; transform: scaleY(1.05); opacity:1;  filter: drop-shadow(0 0 10px #00ffcc); }
  65%  { stroke: #3388ff; stroke-width: 9; transform: scaleY(1.3); opacity:1;   filter: drop-shadow(0 0 16px #3388ff); }
  80%  { stroke: #cc44ff; stroke-width: 4; transform: scaleY(0.8); opacity:0.9; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 6px #cc44ff); }
  100% { stroke: #ff2266; stroke-width: 2; transform: scaleY(0.4); opacity:0.6; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 2px #ff2266); }
}
.ring {
  animation: ringPulse 4s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) infinite;
}
.r1 { animation-delay: 0s; }
.r2 { animation-delay: 0.2s; }
.r3 { animation-delay: 0.4s; }
@keyframes ringPulse {
  0%   { stroke: #ff2266; stroke-width: 1; opacity: 0.3; transform: scale(0.5); }
  25%  { stroke: #ffaa00; stroke-width: 3; opacity: 0.9; transform: scale(1.1); }
  50%  { stroke: #00ffcc; stroke-width: 5; opacity: 1;   transform: scale(1.4); }
  75%  { stroke: #6644ff; stroke-width: 2; opacity: 0.7; transform: scale(1.1); }
  100% { stroke: #ff2266; stroke-width: 1; opacity: 0.3; transform: scale(0.5); }
}
.core {
  animation: corePulse 4s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) infinite;
}
@keyframes corePulse {
  0%   { r: 10; opacity: 0.5; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 4px #fff); }
  30%  { r: 22; opacity: 1;   filter: drop-shadow(0 0 20px #ffffff) drop-shadow(0 0 40px #aa44ff); }
  55%  { r: 16; opacity: 0.8; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 10px #cc88ff); }
  75%  { r: 26; opacity: 1;   filter: drop-shadow(0 0 28px #ffffff) drop-shadow(0 0 56px #6600ff); }
  100% { r: 10; opacity: 0.5; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 4px #fff); }
}
</style>

A text path where individual characters clip-reveal along a curved SVG trajectory while the background behind them shifts position diagonally, the clip region's border-radius morphs from sharp corners to fully rounded and back, and the shadow beneath the text grows darker then lighter in sync with the border-radius phases to enhance the dimensional reveal effect.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg">
  <defs>
    <path id="curve" d="M 20,200 Q 150,40 280,200"/>
    <clipPath id="revealClip">
      <rect class="clip-rect" x="0" y="0" width="300" height="300"/>
    </clipPath>
  </defs>
  <rect class="bg-shift" x="-60" y="-60" width="420" height="420"/>
  <g clip-path="url(#revealClip)">
    <g class="text-shadow-group">
      <text class="curved-text shadow-text">
        <textPath href="#curve" startOffset="0%">WAVE·MOTION·FLOW</textPath>
      </text>
    </g>
    <text class="curved-text main-text">
      <textPath href="#curve" startOffset="0%">WAVE·MOTION·FLOW</textPath>
    </text>
  </g>
</svg>
<style>
.bg-shift {
  fill: #0a0a2e;
  animation: bgDiag 5s ease-in-out infinite;
}
@keyframes bgDiag {
  0%   { transform: translate(0px, 0px);   fill: #0a0a2e; }
  25%  { transform: translate(-20px,-15px); fill: #12103a; }
  50%  { transform: translate(-40px,-30px); fill: #1a0a3e; }
  75%  { transform: translate(-20px,-15px); fill: #12103a; }
  100% { transform: translate(0px, 0px);   fill: #0a0a2e; }
}
.clip-rect {
  animation: clipReveal 5s cubic-bezier(0.6,0,0.4,1) infinite;
  rx: 0;
  ry: 0;
}
@keyframes clipReveal {
  0%   { x: -300px; width: 300px; rx: 0;   ry: 0;   }
  20%  { x: -60px;  width: 300px; rx: 8px;  ry: 8px;  }
  40%  { x: 60px;   width: 300px; rx: 30px; ry: 30px; }
  60%  { x: 160px;  width: 300px; rx: 60px; ry: 60px; }
  75%  { x: 220px;  width: 300px; rx: 30px; ry: 30px; }
  88%  { x: 270px;  width: 300px; rx: 8px;  ry: 8px;  }
  100% { x: 300px;  width: 300px; rx: 0;   ry: 0;   }
}
.curved-text {
  font-family: 'Arial Black', sans-serif;
  font-size: 22px;
  font-weight: 900;
  letter-spacing: 2px;
}
.main-text {
  fill: none;
  stroke: #e0ccff;
  stroke-width: 0.5;
  paint-order: stroke fill;
  fill: #ffffff;
  animation: textColor 5s ease-in-out infinite;
}
@keyframes textColor {
  0%   { fill: #ccaaff; stroke: #8844cc; }
  25%  { fill: #ffffff; stroke: #aa66ff; }
  50%  { fill: #ffe0ff; stroke: #ff88ff; }
  75%  { fill: #ffffff; stroke: #aa66ff; }
  100% { fill: #ccaaff; stroke: #8844cc; }
}
.shadow-text {
  fill: rgba(0,0,0,0);
  stroke: rgba(0,0,0,0);
  filter: drop-shadow(0 4px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.9));
  animation: shadowPulse 5s ease-in-out infinite;
}
@keyframes shadowPulse {
  0%   { filter: drop-shadow(0 2px 4px rgba(80,0,120,0.3)); }
  25%  { filter: drop-shadow(0 5px 12px rgba(80,0,120,0.7)); }
  50%  { filter: drop-shadow(0 8px 22px rgba(120,0,180,1)) drop-shadow(0 0 8px rgba(180,100,255,0.6)); }
  75%  { filter: drop-shadow(0 5px 12px rgba(80,0,120,0.7)); }
  100% { filter: drop-shadow(0 2px 4px rgba(80,0,120,0.3)); }
}
</style>

A liquid blob that morphs through four distinct shapes while its border-radius cycles from 20% to 60% to 40%, its stroke animates as a chasing dashed outline that reverses direction every half-cycle, stroke color transitions from teal to magenta, and an inner shadow inverts intensity in sync with each shape transition.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <defs>
    <filter id="innerShadow">
      <feGaussianBlur in="SourceGraphic" stdDeviation="3" />
      <feOffset dx="0" dy="2" result="offsetblur" />
      <feComponentTransfer>
        <feFuncA type="linear" slope="0.4" />
      </feComponentTransfer>
    </filter>
  </defs>
  <g class="blob-container">
    <circle class="blob" cx="150" cy="150" r="60" fill="none" stroke="#00d4d4" stroke-width="3" filter="url(#innerShadow)" />
  </g>
</svg>
<style>
svg {
  width: 300px;
  height: 300px;
}

.blob {
  animation: blobMorph 4s ease-in-out infinite, blobStroke 4s ease-in-out infinite, blobColor 4s ease-in-out infinite, blobShadow 4s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes blobMorph {
  0% {
    border-radius: 20%;
    rx: 30%;
    ry: 20%;
  }
  25% {
    border-radius: 60%;
    rx: 60%;
    ry: 60%;
  }
  50% {
    border-radius: 40%;
    rx: 40%;
    ry: 50%;
  }
  75% {
    border-radius: 30%;
    rx: 25%;
    ry: 45%;
  }
  100% {
    border-radius: 20%;
    rx: 30%;
    ry: 20%;
  }
}

@keyframes blobStroke {
  0% {
    stroke-dasharray: 376;
    stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  }
  25% {
    stroke-dasharray: 376;
    stroke-dashoffset: -188;
  }
  50% {
    stroke-dasharray: 376;
    stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  }
  75% {
    stroke-dasharray: 376;
    stroke-dashoffset: -188;
  }
  100% {
    stroke-dasharray: 376;
    stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  }
}

@keyframes blobColor {
  0% {
    stroke: #00d4d4;
  }
  50% {
    stroke: #ff00ff;
  }
  100% {
    stroke: #00d4d4;
  }
}

@keyframes blobShadow {
  0% {
    filter: drop-shadow(0 0 0px rgba(0,212,212,0.8));
  }
  50% {
    filter: drop-shadow(0 0 8px rgba(255,0,255,0.4));
  }
  100% {
    filter: drop-shadow(0 0 0px rgba(0,212,212,0.8));
  }
}
</style>

A bouncing ball that scales vertically while squashing (reducing height, increasing width) on impact, the impact zone clips a burst radius that shrinks and expands, the ball's shadow stretches horizontally beneath it during flight then compresses on landing, and a surrounding glow's stroke-width pulses to accentuate each bounce in a six-phase sequence.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <defs>
    <filter id="ballGlow">
      <feGaussianBlur in="SourceGraphic" stdDeviation="2" />
    </filter>
  </defs>
  <g class="ball-scene">
    <ellipse class="ball-shadow" cx="150" cy="260" rx="40" ry="8" fill="rgba(0,0,0,0.3)" />
    <circle class="ball-glow" cx="150" cy="100" r="50" fill="none" stroke="#ff6b9d" stroke-width="2" />
    <circle class="ball" cx="150" cy="100" r="20" fill="#ff6b9d" filter="url(#ballGlow)" />
    <circle class="burst-clip" cx="150" cy="100" r="30" fill="none" stroke="rgba(255,107,157,0.4)" stroke-width="2" />
  </g>
</svg>
<style>
svg {
  width: 300px;
  height: 300px;
}

.ball-scene {
  animation: ballBounce 1.5s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) infinite;
}

.ball {
  animation: ballSquash 1.5s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) infinite;
}

.ball-shadow {
  animation: shadowStretch 1.5s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) infinite;
}

.burst-clip {
  animation: burstPulse 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.ball-glow {
  animation: glowPulse 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes ballBounce {
  0%, 100% {
    transform: translateY(0);
  }
  50% {
    transform: translateY(-120px);
  }
}

@keyframes ballSquash {
  0%, 100% {
    transform: scaleY(1) scaleX(1);
  }
  50% {
    transform: scaleY(1) scaleX(1);
  }
  75% {
    transform: scaleY(0.7) scaleX(1.3);
  }
}

@keyframes shadowStretch {
  0%, 100% {
    rx: 40;
    ry: 8;
  }
  50% {
    rx: 25;
    ry: 12;
  }
  75% {
    rx: 50;
    ry: 6;
  }
}

@keyframes burstPulse {
  0%, 100% {
    r: 30;
    opacity: 0.2;
  }
  16.67% {
    r: 45;
    opacity: 0.4;
  }
  33.33% {
    r: 25;
    opacity: 0.3;
  }
  50% {
    r: 40;
    opacity: 0.5;
  }
  66.67% {
    r: 20;
    opacity: 0.2;
  }
  83.33% {
    r: 35;
    opacity: 0.4;
  }
}

@keyframes glowPulse {
  0%, 100% {
    stroke-width: 2;
  }
  16.67% {
    stroke-width: 4;
  }
  33.33% {
    stroke-width: 2;
  }
  50% {
    stroke-width: 5;
  }
  66.67% {
    stroke-width: 2;
  }
  83.33% {
    stroke-width: 4;
  }
}
</style>

An origami fold sequence where two triangular flaps skew inward from opposite sides while their stroke-dasharray reveals crease lines that animate in staggered phases, the fold depth intensifies through color shifts from white to deep gray, and a shadow beneath the fold grows darker then lighter as the fold tightens then releases.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <g class="origami-fold">
    <polygon class="flap flap-left" points="150,150 80,80 80,220" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="2" />
    <polygon class="flap flap-right" points="150,150 220,80 220,220" fill="none" stroke="#ffffff" stroke-width="2" />
    <line class="crease crease-left" x1="150" y1="150" x2="80" y2="150" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="1" />
    <line class="crease crease-right" x1="150" y1="150" x2="220" y2="150" stroke="#cccccc" stroke-width="1" />
    <ellipse class="fold-shadow" cx="150" cy="200" rx="60" ry="15" fill="rgba(0,0,0,0.2)" />
  </g>
</svg>
<style>
svg {
  width: 300px;
  height: 300px;
}

.flap-left {
  animation: flapLeftSkew 3s ease-in-out infinite, flapColor 3s ease-in-out infinite;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

.flap-right {
  animation: flapRightSkew 3s ease-in-out infinite, flapColor 3s ease-in-out infinite;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

.crease-left {
  animation: creaseLeftDash 3s ease-in-out infinite 0.1s;
}

.crease-right {
  animation: creaseRightDash 3s ease-in-out infinite 0.2s;
}

.fold-shadow {
  animation: shadowIntensity 3s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes flapLeftSkew {
  0%, 100% {
    transform: skewX(0deg);
  }
  50% {
    transform: skewX(25deg);
  }
}

@keyframes flapRightSkew {
  0%, 100% {
    transform: skewX(0deg);
  }
  50% {
    transform: skewX(-25deg);
  }
}

@keyframes flapColor {
  0%, 100% {
    stroke: #ffffff;
  }
  50% {
    stroke: #333333;
  }
}

@keyframes creaseLeftDash {
  0%, 100% {
    stroke-dasharray: 70;
    stroke-dashoffset: 70;
  }
  50% {
    stroke-dasharray: 70;
    stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  }
}

@keyframes creaseRightDash {
  0%, 100% {
    stroke-dasharray: 70;
    stroke-dashoffset: 70;
  }
  50% {
    stroke-dasharray: 70;
    stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  }
}

@keyframes shadowIntensity {
  0%, 100% {
    fill: rgba(0,0,0,0.1);
  }
  50% {
    fill: rgba(0,0,0,0.4);
  }
}
</style>

A segmented loading spinner with eight arcs where each arc's stroke-dasharray chases around its perimeter at staggered 80ms intervals, stroke color ripples through a gradient from indigo to cyan, the arc's stroke-width scales in response to rotation angle, and a central shadow pulses to indicate processing intensity.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="arcGrad" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">
      <stop offset="0%" style="stop-color:#4a00e0;stop-opacity:1" />
      <stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#00d4ff;stop-opacity:1" />
    </linearGradient>
  </defs>
  <g class="spinner-container">
    <circle class="center-pulse" cx="150" cy="150" r="8" fill="rgba(74,0,224,0.3)" />
    <circle class="arc arc-0" cx="150" cy="150" r="60" fill="none" stroke="url(#arcGrad)" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <circle class="arc arc-1" cx="150" cy="150" r="60" fill="none" stroke="url(#arcGrad)" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <circle class="arc arc-2" cx="150" cy="150" r="60" fill="none" stroke="url(#arcGrad)" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <circle class="arc arc-3" cx="150" cy="150" r="60" fill="none" stroke="url(#arcGrad)" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <circle class="arc arc-4" cx="150" cy="150" r="60" fill="none" stroke="url(#arcGrad)" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <circle class="arc arc-5" cx="150" cy="150" r="60" fill="none" stroke="url(#arcGrad)" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <circle class="arc arc-6" cx="150" cy="150" r="60" fill="none" stroke="url(#arcGrad)" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
    <circle class="arc arc-7" cx="150" cy="150" r="60" fill="none" stroke="url(#arcGrad)" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" />
  </g>
</svg>
<style>
svg {
  width: 300px;
  height: 300px;
}

.spinner-container {
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

.center-pulse {
  animation: centerPulse 2s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.arc {
  stroke-dasharray: 94;
  stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

.arc-0 {
  animation: arcChase 2s linear infinite, arcWidth 2s ease-in-out infinite;
  animation-delay: 0ms, 0ms;
}

.arc-1 {
  animation: arcChase 2s linear infinite 0.08s, arcWidth 2s ease-in-out infinite 0.08s;
}

.arc-2 {
  animation: arcChase 2s linear infinite 0.16s, arcWidth 2s ease-in-out infinite 0.16s;
}

.arc-3 {
  animation: arcChase 2s linear infinite 0.24s, arcWidth 2s ease-in-out infinite 0.24s;
}

.arc-4 {
  animation: arcChase 2s linear infinite 0.32s, arcWidth 2s ease-in-out infinite 0.32s;
}

.arc-5 {
  animation: arcChase 2s linear infinite 0.4s, arcWidth 2s ease-in-out infinite 0.4s;
}

.arc-6 {
  animation: arcChase 2s linear infinite 0.48s, arcWidth 2s ease-in-out infinite 0.48s;
}

.arc-7 {
  animation: arcChase 2s linear infinite 0.56s, arcWidth 2s ease-in-out infinite 0.56s;
}

@keyframes arcChase {
  0% {
    stroke-dashoffset: 0;
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
  100% {
    stroke-dashoffset: -94;
    transform: rotate(360deg);
  }
}

@keyframes arcWidth {
  0%, 100% {
    stroke-width: 4;
  }
  25% {
    stroke-width: 6;
  }
  50% {
    stroke-width: 3;
  }
  75% {
    stroke-width: 5;
  }
}

@keyframes centerPulse {
  0%, 100% {
    r: 8;
    fill: rgba(74,0,224,0.2);
  }
  50% {
    r: 12;

Matrix-style falling glyphs that translate downward in staggered columns, each glyph's stroke animates as a glowing outline that brightens then dims, the glyph color shifts from bright green through muted tones, border-radius morphs from sharp to slightly rounded to suggest digital decay, and background-position scrolls vertically beneath to create depth parallax.

Interlocking gears where the primary gear rotates clockwise while three secondary gears counter-rotate, each gear's stroke-width modulates inversely with rotation speed, stroke color cycles through complementary pairs, and skewed perspective shadows beneath each gear shift angle to exaggerate the mechanical interlocking motion across four staggered startup phases.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" style="width: 300px; height: 300px;">

A spiral that expands outward while rotating, its svg stroke-dasharray animates to chase the rotation, the dash pattern accelerates then decelerates creating a breathing rhythm, stroke color gradually shifts from cool blue to warm orange, the shadow radiates and contracts concentrically, and the center clips a rotating mask that obscures and reveals the spiral's core in six staggered cycles.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" style="width: 300px; height: 300px;">
  <defs>
    <filter id="spiralshadow">
      <feGaussianBlur in="SourceAlpha" stdDeviation="2"/>
      <feOffset dx="1" dy="2"/>
      <feComponentTransfer>
        <feFuncA type="linear" slope="0.35"/>
      </feComponentTransfer>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode/>
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
    <radialGradient id="radialShadow" cx="50%" cy="50%" r="50%">
      <stop offset="0%" style="stop-color:#000;stop-opacity:0"/>
      <stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#000;stop-opacity:0.4"/>
    </radialGradient>
    <mask id="spiralMask">
      <circle cx="150" cy="150" r="100" fill="white"/>
      <circle class="mask-circle" cx="150" cy="150" r="30" fill="black"/>
    </mask>
  </defs>
  
  <circle class="spiral-shadow" cx="150" cy="150" r="85" fill="url(#radialShadow)" filter="url(#spiralshadow)"/>
  
  <g class="spiral-group" mask="url(#spiralMask)">
    <path class="spiral-path" d="M 150,150 Q 170,140 180,160 T 200,200 T 180,240 T 140,250 T 100,230 T 80,180 T 100,130 T 150,120" 
          fill="none" stroke="#2563eb" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  </g>
  
  <circle class="mask-circle-animate" cx="150" cy="150" r="30" fill="none" stroke="none"/>
</svg>

<style>
.spiral-group {
  animation: spiralRotate 6s linear infinite, spiralExpand 6s ease-in-out infinite;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

.spiral-path {
  stroke-dasharray: 200;
  stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  animation: spiralDash 6s ease-in-out infinite,
             spiralColor 6s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.spiral-shadow {
  animation: shadowRadiate 6s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.mask-circle-animate {
  animation: maskRotate 1s linear infinite;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

@keyframes spiralRotate {
  from {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
  to {
    transform: rotate(360deg);
  }
}

@keyframes spiralExpand {
  0% {
    transform: rotate(0deg) scale(0.8);
  }
  50% {
    transform: rotate(180deg) scale(1.2);
  }
  100% {
    transform: rotate(360deg) scale(0.8);
  }
}

@keyframes spiralDash {
  0% {
    stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  }
  25% {
    stroke-dashoffset: 50;
  }
  50% {
    stroke-dashoffset: 100;
  }
  75% {
    stroke-dashoffset: 50;
  }
  100% {
    stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  }
}

@keyframes spiralColor {
  0% {
    stroke: #2563eb;
  }
  50% {
    stroke: #f97316;
  }
  100% {
    stroke: #2563eb;
  }
}

@keyframes shadowRadiate {
  0% {
    r: 75px;
    opacity: 0.2;
  }
  50% {
    r: 95px;
    opacity: 0.5;
  }
  100% {
    r: 75px;
    opacity: 0.2;
  }
}

@keyframes maskRotate {
  from {
    transform: rotate(0deg);
  }
  to {
    transform: rotate(360deg);
  }
}
</style>

A pulsing DNA helix where two intertwined curves skew and counter-skew on alternating axes, each strand's stroke-width oscillates inversely (one fattens as the other thins), stroke-dasharray chases in opposite directions at different speeds, color gradients flow through complementary spectrums, and shadows cast at opposing angles to amplify the helical depth across eight staggered timing phases.

<svg width="300" height="300" viewBox="0 0 300 300">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="grad1" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">

      <stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#00ffff;stop-opacity:1" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="grad2" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">

      <stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#ff00ff;stop-opacity:1" />

      <feGaussianBlur in="SourceAlpha" stdDeviation="3"/>

      <feComponentTransfer>

        <feMergeNode/>

  <g class="helix-group">
    <path class="strand strand1" d="M 80 50 Q 150 80 150 150 Q 150 220 80 250" fill="none" stroke="url(#grad1)" stroke-width="8" filter="url(#shadow1)"/>
    <path class="strand strand2" d="M 220 50 Q 150 80 150 150 Q 150 220 220 250" fill="none" stroke="url(#grad2)" stroke-width="8" filter="url(#shadow2)"/>
  </g>
</svg>
<style>

.strand {
  stroke-linecap: round;

A radiant sun where central rays rotate while their stroke-width scales rhythmically, the rays' stroke color transitions from yellow through orange to red in a three-phase heating sequence, a surrounding halo's border-radius morphs from sharp to completely rounded and back, and the outer glow's shadow intensifies as the animation peaks, all with staggered timing for each ray group.

<style>

  animation-delay: 0.3s;

  to { transform: rotate(360deg); }

@keyframes ray-color {

}

A water droplet that falls and bounces, its clip-path shifts from teardrop to sphere on impact, the droplet's stroke animates as a surface tension line that moves across its surface, stroke color fades from saturated blue to pale, the shadow beneath stretches horizontally on landing then snaps back, and the background-position shifts upward to simulate water reflection parallax.

A kaleidoscope pattern where twelve triangular wedges rotate clockwise while skewing on alternating axes, each wedge's stroke-width pulsates to the beat, stroke color sequences through a rainbow gradient in staggered phases (offset by 30ms per wedge), the center clips a rotating mask with morphing border-radius, and layered shadows cast at varying opacity to create depth.

<svg width="300" height="300" viewBox="0 0 300 300">

</svg>
<style>

  animation: kaleido-rotate 8s linear infinite;

             wedge-color 6s ease-in-out infinite;

             wedge-color 6s ease-in-out infinite 0.03s;

  animation: wedge-width 4s ease-in-out infinite 0.06s,
             wedge-color 6s ease-in-out infinite 0.06s;

             wedge-color 6s ease-in-out infinite 0.09s;

A pulsating membrane that cycles border-radius from 10% (sharp) to 50% (bulbous) while its stroke-dasharray animates as a chasing wave, the wave acceleration creates a four-phase rhythm (slow-fast-slow-medium), stroke color shifts from pale to vibrant and back, and the membrane's shadow inverts (cast outward then inward) to suggest expansion and contraction.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">

A geometric tessellation where six hexagons translate inward toward center in staggered sequence, each hexagon's stroke-width scales as it approaches center (fattening on arrival), stroke color builds from muted to saturated as shapes converge, border-radius subtly morphs to create bulging effects, and overlapping shadows darken to suggest layering depth.

A folding envelope where the top flap skews downward while its stroke-dasharray animates to trace a crease line, the flap's stroke color shifts from white to gray as it closes, the back panel's shadow grows darker beneath the flap, the envelope's border-radius subtly rounds corners on open then sharpens on close, and the background-position shifts to simulate paper texture translation.

A rotating aperture where concentric rings scale outward while counter-rotating, each ring's stroke-dasharray animates in opposite directions, stroke color sequences through complementary gradients in staggered phases, inner shadows alternate between sharp and diffuse, and the central clip-path shrinks then expands to create a pulsing iris effect across six nested ring layers.

Stacked cards that fan outward from center while each card's border-radius rounds progressively as it rotates, the top card scales up slightly during the spread, shadows cast at increasing angles beneath each card, and a background-position shift creates a subtle depth parallax as cards separate.

A ferris wheel where passenger pods rotate around a central hub while their border-radius cycles from sharp rectangles to rounded capsules and back, each pod's stroke-dasharray animates to trace window outlines in staggered sequence, and shadows beneath pods lengthen and compress based on rotational position to simulate three-dimensional perspective.

A wave of falling dominoes where each block translates downward in staggered sequence while skewing forward on impact, the block's border-radius subtly rounds on collision, the stroke-width fattens at contact point, and the shadow beneath stretches horizontally then snaps back, creating a cascading topple effect.

A swinging pendulum bob that rotates on an arc while its border-radius cycles from sharp at the swing extremes to completely rounded at the center point, creating a breathing effect; the bob's stroke animates as a fading trail, the shadow beneath stretches perpendicular to motion, and the swing's scale compresses slightly at peak velocity.

A geometric spiral expanding outward while rotating clockwise, its stroke-dasharray accelerates then decelerates in a breathing rhythm, stroke color gradually shifts from cool blue to warm orange across three seconds, the spiral's border-radius at curve intersections morphs from sharp to rounded, and a concentric shadow radiates and contracts to create depth.

<svg class="spiral-container" viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <g class="spiral-group">

    <ellipse class="spiral-shadow" cx="150" cy="150" rx="80" ry="80" opacity="0.15"/>

<style>
.spiral-container {

.spiral-path {
  stroke: #4a90e2;
  stroke-width: 3;
  fill: none;
  stroke-linecap: round;
  stroke-dasharray: 400;
  stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  animation: colorShift 3s ease-in-out infinite, dashBreathe 3s ease-in-out infinite;

.spiral-shadow {

  animation: shadowRadiate 3s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes spiralRotate {
  0% { transform: rotate(0deg) scale(0.8); }
  50% { transform: rotate(180deg) scale(1.1); }
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg) scale(0.8); }
}

@keyframes colorShift {
  0% { stroke: #4a90e2; }
  50% { stroke: #ff8a65; }
  100% { stroke: #4a90e2; }
}

@keyframes dashBreathe {
  0%, 100% { stroke-dashoffset: 0; }
  50% { stroke-dashoffset: 200; }
}

@keyframes shadowRadiate {
  0%, 100% { rx: 60; ry: 60; opacity: 0.08; }
  50% { rx: 100; ry: 100; opacity: 0.2; }
}
</style>

A pulsing orb where the central sphere scales up and down while its border-radius cycles from slightly rounded (20%) to completely smooth (50%) in sync with expansion, the outer glow's stroke-width pulses inversely to scale, stroke color brightens on expansion then dims on contraction, and layered shadows grow and shrink to emphasize the breathing effect.

Six geometric petals that scale outward from center while rotating clockwise, each petal's stroke animates with a dashing wave that ripples outward in staggered 100ms offsets, stroke color sequences through a rainbow gradient, and the petal's border-radius morphs from sharp angular points to soft rounded lobes as scale increases, with a central expanding shadow creating concentric rings.

A liquid blob morphing through four distinct shapes while its border-radius cycles between 30% (angular) and 60% (bulbous), its outline stroke-dasharray chases in reverse direction every half-cycle, stroke color transitions from teal to magenta, and an inner shadow alternates between sharp and soft to suggest surface tension shifts and dimensional morphing.

A bouncing basketball that scales vertically while squashing horizontally on impact, the impact zone clips a burst-pattern that expands then contracts, the ball's shadow stretches during flight and compresses on landing, and a surrounding glow's stroke-width pulses to accentuate each bounce across six phases with staggered timing for secondary bounces.

An origami fold where two triangular flaps skew inward from opposite sides while their stroke-dasharray reveals crease lines in staggered animation phases, the fold depth intensifies through color shifts from white to deep gray, the folds' stroke-width increases as they close, and a shadow beneath grows darker as the fold tightens, suggesting compressed three-dimensional paper layering.

A segmented loading spinner with eight arcs where each arc's stroke-dasharray chases around its perimeter at staggered 80ms intervals, stroke color ripples through indigo-to-cyan gradient with each arc offset by 50ms, the arc's stroke-width scales responsively to rotation angle, and a central shadow pulses to indicate processing intensity in four rapid phases.

A paper airplane that translates upward along a curved arc while rotating on three axes, its fuselage stroke-dasharray animates as if wind-peeling the outline backwards, the plane's clip-path narrows and widens to simulate banking turns, the shadow beneath stretches at sharp angles during turns then compresses in straight flight, and the background-position shifts to create wind-swept depth.

Matrix-style falling glyphs translating downward in staggered columns, each glyph's stroke animates as a glowing outline that brightens then dims in three-phase cycles, the glyph color shifts from bright green through faded tones to suggest digital decay, the glyph's border-radius subtly morphs from sharp to slightly rounded as opacity decreases, and background-position scrolls vertically beneath creating depth.

Three interlocking gears where the primary gear rotates clockwise while two secondary gears counter-rotate, each gear's stroke-width modulates inversely with rotation speed, stroke color cycles through complementary gold-and-purple pairs, and skewed perspective shadows shift angle beneath each gear to exaggerate mechanical interlocking across four staggered startup acceleration phases.

      <rect class="tooth" x="75" y="150" width="10" height="10" transform="rotate(180 80 180)"/>

  </g>

    <g class="gear-teeth">
      <rect class="tooth" x="215" y="150" width="10" height="10"/>

<style>
.gears-container {
  width: 300px;
  height: 300px;
}

.gear-shadow {
  fill: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.2);

}

.gear-body {

  stroke: #d4af37;
  stroke-width: 3;

}

.tooth {

  animation: colorCycle 2s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.primary .gear-body {
  animation: rotatePrimary 2s linear infinite, gearStrokeW 2s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.primary .gear-teeth {
  animation: rotatePrimary 2s linear infinite;
}

.secondary-left .gear-body,
.secondary-left .gear-teeth {
  animation: rotateSecondary 2s linear infinite, colorCycleSecondary 2s ease-in-out infinite 0.25s;
  transform-origin: 80px 180px;
}

.secondary-right .gear-body,
.secondary-right .gear-teeth {
  animation: rotateSecondary 2s linear infinite, colorCycleSecondary 2s ease-in-out infinite 0.5s;
  transform-origin: 220px 180px;
}

@keyframes rotatePrimary {
  0% { transform: rotate(0deg) scaleY(1); }
  25% { transform: rotate(90deg) scaleY(1); }
  50% { transform: rotate(180deg) scaleY(1); }

}

@keyframes rotateSecondary {

  25% { transform: rotate(-90deg) scaleY(1); }
  50% { transform: rotate(-180deg) scaleY(1); }
  75% { transform: rotate(-270deg) scaleY(1); }
  100% { transform: rotate(-360deg) scaleY(1); }
}

@keyframes gearStrokeW {

  50% { stroke-width: 1.5; }
}

@keyframes colorCycle {
  0%, 100% { fill: #d4af37; }
  50% { fill: #9945ff; }
}

@keyframes colorCycleSecondary {
  0%, 100% { fill: #9945ff; }
  50% { fill: #d4af37; }
}

@keyframes shadowSkew {
  0%, 100% { transform: skewX(0deg); }
  25% { transform: skewX(15deg); }
  50% { transform: skewX(0deg); }
  75% { transform: skewX(-15deg); }
}
</style>

A DNA double helix where two intertwined curves skew and counter-skew on alternating axes while rotating, each strand's stroke-width oscillates inversely (one fattens as the other thins), stroke-dasharray chases in opposite directions at different speeds, color gradients flow through complementary blue-to-orange spectrums in staggered phase offsets, and shadows cast at opposing angles amplify helical three-dimensional depth.

<svg class="dna-helix" viewBox="0 0 300 400" width="120" height="300">
  <defs>
    <filter id="dnaGlow">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="2"/>
    </filter>
  </defs>
  
  <g class="dna-container">
    <ellipse class="shadow-left" cx="80" cy="200" rx="25" ry="8" filter="url(#dnaGlow)"/>
    <ellipse class="shadow-right" cx="220" cy="200" rx="25" ry="8" filter="url(#dnaGlow)"/>
    
    <path class="strand strand-1" d="M 80 50 Q 120 100, 140 150 Q 120 200, 80 250 Q 120 300, 140 350"/>
    <path class="strand strand-2" d="M 220 50 Q 180 100, 160 150 Q 180 200, 220 250 Q 180 300, 160 350"/>
    
    <g class="connectors">
      <line class="connector" x1="95" y1="80" x2="205" y2="80"/>
      <line class="connector" x1="100" y1="130" x2="200" y2="130"/>
      <line class="connector" x1="105" y1="180" x2="195" y2="180"/>
      <line class="connector" x1="100" y1="230" x2="200" y2="230"/>
      <line class="connector" x1="95" y1="280" x2="205" y2="280"/>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>

<style>
.dna-helix {
  width: 300px;
  height: 300px;
}

.dna-container {
  animation: helixRotate 3s linear infinite;
  transform-origin: 150px 200px;
}

.shadow-left {
  fill: rgba(0, 150, 255, 0.15);
  animation: shadowSkew 3s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.shadow-right {
  fill: rgba(255, 100, 0, 0.15);
  animation: shadowSkewInverse 3s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.strand {
  fill: none;
  stroke-linecap: round;
  stroke-width: 3;
  stroke-dasharray: 80;
  filter: drop-shadow(0 0 4px currentColor);
}

.strand-1 {
  stroke: url(#grad1);
  animation: dash1 2s linear infinite, colorGrad1 3s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.strand-2 {
  stroke: url(#grad2);
  animation: dash2 2s linear infinite 0.5s, colorGrad2 3s ease-in-out infinite 1s;
}

.connector {
  stroke: #aaa;
  stroke-width: 1.5;
  animation: connectorPulse 3s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes helixRotate {
  0% { transform: rotateX(0deg) rotateY(0deg); }
  50% { transform: rotateX(25deg) rotateY(360deg); }
  100% { transform: rotateX(0deg) rotateY(720deg); }
}

@keyframes dash1 {
  0% { stroke-dashoffset: 0; }
  100% { stroke-dashoffset: 80; }
}

@keyframes dash2 {
  0% { stroke-dashoffset: 0; }
  100% { stroke-dashoffset: -80; }
}

@keyframes colorGrad1 {
  0%, 100% { stroke: #0096ff; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 4px #0096ff); }
  50% { stroke: #ff6400; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 6px #ff6400); }
}

@keyframes colorGrad2 {
  0%, 100% { stroke: #ff6400; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 4px #ff6400); }
  50% { stroke: #0096ff; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 6px #0096ff); }
}

@keyframes shadowSkew {
  0%, 100% { transform: skewX(-15deg); }
  50% { transform: skewX(15deg); }
}

@keyframes shadowSkewInverse {
  0%, 100% { transform: skewX(15deg); }
  50% { transform: skewX(-15deg); }
}

@keyframes connectorPulse {
  0%, 100% { stroke: #aaa; opacity: 0.6; stroke-width: 1.5; }
  50% { stroke: #fff; opacity: 1; stroke-width: 2; }
}

svg {
  --grad1: linear-gradient(90deg, #0096ff, #ff6400);
  --grad2: linear-gradient(90deg, #ff6400, #0096ff);
}
</style>

A radiant sun where six rays rotate outward from center while their stroke-width scales rhythmically, stroke color transitions from yellow through orange to deep red in a three-phase heating sequence, a surrounding halo's border-radius morphs from razor-sharp (0%) to completely rounded (50%) and back, the outer glow's shadow intensifies at peak animation, and each ray group rotates at staggered timing creating a cascading radiance effect.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <defs>
    <filter id="sunGlow">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="3" result="coloredBlur"/>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in="coloredBlur"/>
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>
  
  <circle class="halo" cx="150" cy="150" r="80" fill="none" stroke="rgba(255,200,0,0.3)" stroke-width="2"/>
  
  <g class="rays rays1">
    <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="50" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round" filter="url(#sunGlow)"/>
    <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="50" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round" filter="url(#sunGlow)" transform="rotate(60 150 150)"/>
    <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="50" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round" filter="url(#sunGlow)" transform="rotate(120 150 150)"/>
  </g>
  
  <g class="rays rays2">
    <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="40" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" filter="url(#sunGlow)"/>
    <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="40" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" filter="url(#sunGlow)" transform="rotate(60 150 150)"/>
    <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="40" stroke="currentColor" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round" filter="url(#sunGlow)" transform="rotate(120 150 150)"/>
  </g>
  
  <circle class="core" cx="150" cy="150" r="20" fill="url(#sunGradient)"/>
  
  <defs>
    <radialGradient id="sunGradient">
      <stop offset="0%" style="stop-color:#ffff99;stop-opacity:1" />
      <stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#ff6600;stop-opacity:1" />
    </radialGradient>
  </defs>
</svg>

<style>
svg {
  animation: none;
}

.halo {
  animation: haloMorph 4s ease-in-out infinite;
  filter: drop-shadow(0 0 15px rgba(255,100,0,0.6));
}

.rays1 {
  color: #ffdd00;
  animation: rayRotate 6s linear infinite, rayColor 3s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.rays2 {
  color: #ff8800;
  animation: rayRotate 8s linear infinite reverse, rayStroke 3s ease-in-out infinite 0.5s;
}

.core {
  animation: corePulse 3s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes haloMorph {
  0% { border-radius: 0%; }
  50% { border-radius: 50%; }
  100% { border-radius: 0%; }
}

@keyframes rayRotate {
  from { transform: rotate(0deg); }
  to { transform: rotate(360deg); }
}

@keyframes rayColor {
  0% { color: #ffdd00; }
  33% { color: #ffaa00; }
  66% { color: #cc3300; }
  100% { color: #ffdd00; }
}

@keyframes rayStroke {
  0% { stroke-width: 2px; }
  50% { stroke-width: 6px; }
  100% { stroke-width: 2px; }
}

@keyframes corePulse {
  0% { r: 18px; }
  50% { r: 26px; }
  100% { r: 18px; }
}
</style>

A water droplet that falls and bounces where the clip-path shifts from elongated teardrop to perfect sphere on impact, the droplet's stroke animates as a surface tension line traversing its surface in staggered waves, stroke color fades from saturated blue to pale transparency, the shadow beneath stretches horizontally on landing then snaps back, and background-position shifts upward to simulate reflected water ripples beneath.

A pulsating biological membrane that cycles border-radius from angular (10%) to bulbous (50%) while its stroke-dasharray animates as a chasing wave accelerating through four phases (slow-fast-slow-medium), stroke color shifts from pale pastel to vibrant neon and back, and the membrane's shadow inverts from cast-outward to cast-inward creating visual expansion and contraction tension.

A geometric tessellation where six hexagons translate inward toward center in staggered 150ms sequence, each hexagon's stroke-width scales as it approaches center (fattening on arrival), stroke color builds from muted gray to saturated color as shapes converge, border-radius subtly morphs to create slight bulging effects, and overlapping shadows darken to suggest layering compression depth.

A folding envelope where the top flap skews downward while its stroke-dasharray animates to trace crease lines in three phases, the flap's stroke color shifts from white to gray as it closes, the back panel's shadow grows darker beneath the closing flap, the envelope's border-radius subtly rounds corners when open then sharpens when closed, and background-position shifts subtly to simulate paper texture translation.

A swinging pendulum bob that arcs back and forth while its border-radius cycles from sharp-angled at the swing extremes to completely rounded at center, creating a breathing pulse; the bob's stroke-dasharray animates as a fading motion trail behind it, the shadow beneath stretches perpendicular to the arc motion, and scale compresses slightly at peak velocity.

A starfield warp where concentric circular clips expand and contract in rippling waves while rotating, each clip-path ring reveals layered background gradients shifting position beneath, the outermost clip's border-radius morphs from sharp to rounded and back in sync with expansion phases, and shadows radiate concentrically to create depth tunnel effect.

An origami crease-fold where two triangular flaps skew inward from opposite sides while their stroke-dasharray animates to reveal sharp crease lines in staggered phases, the fold's color shifts from bright white to deep charcoal, stroke-width increases as flaps compress, and the shadow beneath darkens progressively suggesting layered three-dimensional paper density.

A metaball blob cluster that morphs through four distinct bulbous shapes while its stroke animates as a chasing dashed outline reversing direction every half-cycle, border-radius cycles from 35% angular to 65% fully-round, stroke-color transitions through teal-magenta-cyan spectrum, and an inner shadow inverts intensity at each shape transition suggesting dimensional surface tension.

A comet streaking diagonally across the screen while its trailing tail's stroke-dasharray animates backward at a 150ms lag behind the head's movement, the tail's stroke-width fattens then thins across three acceleration phases, the comet head's shadow stretches and compresses perpendicular to motion, and tail color fades from white through amber to transparency.

A lightning arc that strikes downward while its stroke-dasharray animates as a crackling pulse traveling along the arc path, the bolt's stroke-width modulates to suggest electrical intensity peaks, stroke-color flashes from white to purple to blue in rapid staggered bursts, the shadow beside the bolt grows and shrinks, and clip-path masking reveals the arc in jagged reveal-and-conceal patterns.

<svg width="300" height="300" viewBox="0 0 300 300" style="overflow: visible;">
  <defs>

  <path class="lightning-shadow" d="M 150,30 L 145,80 L 160,85 L 155,140 L 170,145 L 160,200 L 155,270"

<style>
.lightning-bolt {

  100% { stroke: white; }

@keyframes widthPulse {

  50% { stroke-width: 3; }

Six geometric petals scaling outward from center in staggered 100ms sequence while rotating clockwise, each petal's stroke animates with a dashing wave rippling outward, stroke color sequences through rainbow spectrum offset by 50ms per petal, border-radius morphs from sharp angular points to soft rounded lobes as scale increases, and a central expanding shadow creates concentric ring depth.

A breathing grid of nine square cells where each cell's border-radius cycles independently from sharp to rounded, the grid's overall size pulses up and down, each cell's stroke-width modulates inversely with pulsing (thin on expand, thick on contract), stroke-color ripples through a wave of hue shifts, and shadows beneath cells deepen then lighten in staggered sequence.

A rotating kaleidoscope pattern where twelve triangular wedges rotate while skewing on alternating axes, each wedge's stroke-width pulsates to a four-beat rhythm, stroke-color sequences through rainbow gradient offset by 30ms per wedge, the center clips a rotating mask with morphing border-radius from sharp to fully-rounded, and layered shadows cast at varying opacity suggest radial depth.

<svg class="kaleidoscope" width="300" height="300" viewBox="0 0 300 300">
  <defs>
    <clipPath id="centerMask">
      <circle class="mask-circle" cx="150" cy="150" r="80"/>
    </clipPath>
  </defs>
  <g class="kaleidoscope-group" clip-path="url(#centerMask)">
    <g class="wedge" style="--index: 0;">
      <polygon points="150,150 150,50 190,150" stroke="#ff0000" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
    </g>
    <g class="wedge" style="--index: 1;">
      <polygon points="150,150 150,50 190,150" stroke="#ff7f00" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
    </g>
    <g class="wedge" style="--index: 2;">
      <polygon points="150,150 150,50 190,150" stroke="#ffff00" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
    </g>
    <g class="wedge" style="--index: 3;">
      <polygon points="150,150 150,50 190,150" stroke="#00ff00" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/>
    </g>
    <g class="wedge" style="--index: 4;">

A glitch datamosh effect where three rectangular layers translate horizontally at staggered speeds while their clip-path apertures shrink and expand out of sync, each layer's stroke-dasharray animates as a horizontal scan line, and stroke-color flashes through red-green-blue channels in rapid bursts to simulate chromatic aberration with a central shadow that splinters and reforms.

A liquid blob that morphs through four distinct amorphous shapes while its border-radius cycles from 25% angular to 65% fully-bulbous, the blob's fill-color transitions through teal-magenta-cyan, a surrounding stroke-dasharray chases the boundary in reverse direction every half-cycle, and an inner shadow inverts from cast-inward to cast-outward suggesting surface tension shifts.

A checkerboard cascade where sixteen square tiles flip on the Y-axis in a staggered wave pattern (each tile offset by 40ms), each tile's border-radius rounds from sharp to slightly curved as it rotates mid-flip, the tile's background-color alternates between two complementary hues, and shadows cast beneath each tile lengthen then compress based on rotation angle.

A starfield warp where six concentric circular clips expand and contract in rippling waves while rotating, each clip-path layer reveals layered background gradients shifting position diagonally beneath in parallax, the outermost clip's border-radius morphs from sharp to rounded and back, and a radial shadow expands then contracts concentrically to create depth tunnel effect.

A morphing gear set where the primary gear rotates clockwise while two secondary gears counter-rotate at staggered speeds, each gear's stroke-width modulates inversely with rotation speed to suggest mechanical tension, stroke-color cycles through gold-purple complementary pairs, and skewed perspective shadows shift angle beneath each gear to exaggerate three-dimensional interlocking engagement.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <defs>
    <filter id="shadow1">
      <feGaussianBlur in="SourceAlpha" stdDeviation="3"/>
      <feOffset dx="2" dy="4" result="offsetblur"/>
      <feComponentTransfer>
        <feFuncA type="linear" slope="0.3"/>
      </feComponentTransfer>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode/>
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>
  <g filter="url(#shadow1)">
    <!-- Primary gear -->
    <circle class="primary-gear" cx="150" cy="150" r="45" fill="none" stroke="#FFD700" stroke-width="8"/>
    <!-- Secondary gear 1 -->
    <circle class="secondary-gear gear1" cx="210" cy="150" r="30" fill="none" stroke="#9370DB" stroke-width="6"/>
    <!-- Secondary gear 2 -->
    <circle class="secondary-gear gear2" cx="90" cy="150" r="28" fill="none" stroke="#FFD700" stroke-width="5"/>
    <!-- Shadows -->
    <ellipse class="shadow shadow1" cx="150" cy="165" rx="48" ry="8" fill="#000" opacity="0.2"/>
    <ellipse class="shadow shadow2" cx="210" cy="165" rx="32" ry="6" fill="#000" opacity="0.2"/>
    <ellipse class="shadow shadow3" cx="90" cy="165" rx="30" ry="6" fill="#000" opacity="0.2"/>
  </g>
</svg>
<style>
.primary-gear {
  animation: rotatePrimary 3s linear infinite;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

.secondary-gear {
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

.gear1 {
  animation: rotateGear1 2s linear infinite, strokeWidth1 2s ease-in-out infinite, strokeColor1 4s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.gear2 {
  animation: rotateGear2 2.4s linear infinite, strokeWidth2 2.4s ease-in-out infinite, strokeColor2 4s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.shadow1 {
  animation: shadowAngle1 3s ease-in-out infinite;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

.shadow2 {
  animation: shadowAngle2 2s ease-in-out infinite;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

.shadow3 {
  animation: shadowAngle3 2.4s ease-in-out infinite;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

@keyframes rotatePrimary {
  from { transform: rotate(0deg); }
  to { transform: rotate(360deg); }
}

@keyframes rotateGear1 {
  from { transform: rotate(0deg); }
  to { transform: rotate(-360deg); }
}

@keyframes rotateGear2 {
  from { transform: rotate(0deg); }
  to { transform: rotate(-360deg); }
}

@keyframes strokeWidth1 {
  0% { stroke-width: 4; }
  50% { stroke-width: 8; }
  100% { stroke-width: 4; }
}

@keyframes strokeWidth2 {
  0% { stroke-width: 3; }
  50% { stroke-width: 6; }
  100% { stroke-width: 3; }
}

@keyframes strokeColor1 {
  0% { stroke: #FFD700; }
  50% { stroke: #9370DB; }
  100% { stroke: #FFD700; }
}

@keyframes strokeColor2 {
  0% { stroke: #9370DB; }
  50% { stroke: #FFD700; }
  100% { stroke: #9370DB; }
}

@keyframes shadowAngle1 {
  0% { transform: rotate(0deg) skewY(5deg); }
  50% { transform: rotate(180deg) skewY(-5deg); }
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg) skewY(5deg); }
}

@keyframes shadowAngle2 {
  0% { transform: rotate(0deg) skewY(8deg); }
  50% { transform: rotate(180deg) skewY(-8deg); }
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg) skewY(8deg); }
}

@keyframes shadowAngle3 {
  0% { transform: rotate(0deg) skewY(-6deg); }
  50% { transform: rotate(180deg) skewY(6deg); }
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg) skewY(-6deg); }
}
</style>

A lightning arc that strikes downward diagonally while its stroke-dasharray animates as a crackling pulse traveling along the arc path, the bolt's stroke-width modulates rhythmically to suggest electrical intensity peaks through three staggered bursts, stroke-color flashes white-to-purple-to-blue in rapid sequence, the shadow beside the bolt stretches and compresses perpendicular to the arc, and clip-path masking reveals the arc in jagged reveal-and-conceal patterns every 200ms.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <defs>
    <filter id="glow">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="2" result="coloredBlur"/>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in="coloredBlur"/>
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
    <clipPath id="boltClip">
      <path d="M 150 50 L 165 120 L 140 130 L 180 250 L 165 240 L 145 180 L 155 170 Z"/>
    </clipPath>
  </defs>
  <g filter="url(#glow)">
    <!-- Shadow -->
    <path class="bolt-shadow" d="M 152 50 L 167 120 L 142 130 L 182 250 L 167 240 L 147 180 L 157 170 Z" 
          fill="none" stroke="rgba(0,0,0,0.3)" stroke-width="8" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <!-- Main bolt -->
    <path class="lightning-bolt" d="M 150 50 L 165 120 L 140 130 L 180 250 L 165 240 L 145 180 L 155 170 Z" 
          fill="none" stroke="#FFFFFF" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
    <!-- Crackling overlay with dash animation -->
    <path class="bolt-crackle" d="M 150 50 L 165 120 L 140 130 L 180 250 L 165 240 L 145 180 L 155 170 Z"
          fill="none" stroke="#FFFFFF" stroke-width="4" stroke-linecap="round"/>
  </g>
</svg>
<style>
.lightning-bolt {
  animation: boltStrike 1.2s ease-in infinite, boltWidth 1.2s ease-in-out infinite, boltColor 0.3s ease-in infinite;
  stroke-dasharray: 300;
  stroke-dashoffset: 0;
}

.bolt-crackle {
  animation: crackle 0.2s step(4, end) infinite;
  stroke: #FFFFFF;
  opacity: 0.7;
}

.bolt-shadow {
  animation: shadowStretch 1.2s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes boltStrike {
  0% { transform: translateY(-20px) translateX(-10px); opacity: 0; }
  5% { opacity: 1; }
  10% { transform: translateY(0) translateX(0); }
  20% { opacity: 0.8; }
  100% { transform: translateY(0) translateX(0); opacity: 0; }
}

@keyframes boltWidth {
  0% { stroke-width: 2; }
  5% { stroke-width: 6; }
  15% { stroke-width: 3; }
  35% { stroke-width: 5; }
  55% { stroke-width: 2; }
  100% { stroke-width: 2; }
}

@keyframes boltColor {
  0% { stroke: #FFFFFF; }
  3% { stroke: #FFFFFF; }
  6% { stroke: #A020F0; }
  9% { stroke: #4169E1; }
  12% { stroke: #FFFFFF; }
  100% { stroke: #FFFFFF; }
}

@keyframes crackle {
  0% { stroke-dashoffset: 0; opacity: 1; }
  25% { stroke-dashoffset: -50; opacity: 0.6; }
  50% { stroke-dashoffset: -100; opacity: 0.3; }
  75% { stroke-dashoffset: -150; opacity: 0.6; }
  100% { stroke-dashoffset: -300; opacity: 0; }
}

@keyframes shadowStretch {
  0% { stroke-width: 6; transform: scaleX(0.8); }
  10% { stroke-width: 12; transform: scaleX(1.2); }
  50% { stroke-width: 8; transform: scaleX(1); }
  100% { stroke-width: 6; transform: scaleX(0.8); }
}
</style>

A whirling vortex where twelve triangular wedges rotate clockwise while skewing inward on alternating axes in staggered 80ms sequence, each wedge's stroke-width pulsates to a four-beat rotation rhythm, stroke-color sequences through full rainbow spectrum offset by 30ms per wedge, the center clips a rotating mask with morphing border-radius from sharp to fully-rounded, and layered shadows cast at varying opacity suggest radial depth recession.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <defs>
    <filter id="vortexShadow">
      <feGaussianBlur in="SourceAlpha" stdDeviation="2"/>
      <feOffset dx="1" dy="2" result="offsetblur"/>
      <feComponentTransfer>
        <feFuncA type="linear" slope="0.25"/>
      </feComponentTransfer>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode/>
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
    <mask id="centerMask">
      <circle class="mask-circle" cx="150" cy="150" r="30" fill="white"/>
    </mask>
  </defs>
  <g filter="url(#vortexShadow)" mask="url(#centerMask)">
    <!-- 12 wedges -->
    <g class="wedge wedge1" style="animation-delay: 0ms;"><polygon points="150,150 165,80 145,80" fill="none" stroke="#FF0000" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge2" style="animation-delay: 80ms;"><polygon points="150,150 195,95 180,110" fill="none" stroke="#FF7F00" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge3" style="animation-delay: 160ms;"><polygon points="150,150 220,150 220,135" fill="none" stroke="#FFFF00" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge4" style="animation-delay: 240ms;"><polygon points="150,150 195,205 180,190" fill="none" stroke="#00FF00" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge5" style="animation-delay: 320ms;"><polygon points="150,150 150,220 165,220" fill="none" stroke="#0000FF" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge6" style="animation-delay: 400ms;"><polygon points="150,150 105,205 120,190" fill="none" stroke="#4B0082" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge7" style="animation-delay: 480ms;"><polygon points="150,150 80,150 80,165" fill="none" stroke="#9400D3" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge8" style="animation-delay: 560ms;"><polygon points="150,150 105,95 120,110" fill="none" stroke="#FF0000" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge9" style="animation-delay: 640ms;"><polygon points="150,150 170,75 155,75" fill="none" stroke="#FF7F00" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge10" style="animation-delay: 720ms;"><polygon points="150,150 225,125 225,140" fill="none" stroke="#FFFF00" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge11" style="animation-delay: 800ms;"><polygon points="150,150 175,225 190,215" fill="none" stroke="#00FF00" stroke-width="4"/></g>
    <g class="wedge wedge12" style="animation-delay: 880ms;"><polygon points="150,150 75,175 75,160" fill="none" stroke="#0000FF" stroke-width="4"/></g>
  </g>
  <!-- Center rotating mask -->
  <circle class="center-core" cx="150" cy="150" r="30" fill="none" stroke="#FFFFFF" stroke-width="2" opacity="0.3"/>
</svg>
<style>
.wedge {
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
  animation: wedgeRotate 2.4s linear infinite, wedgeSkew 2.4s ease-in-out infinite, wedgeWidth 2.4s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.wedge1 { --color-offset: 0; }
.wedge2 { --color-offset: 30ms; }
.wedge3 { --color-offset: 60ms; }
.wedge4 { --color-offset: 90ms; }
.wedge5 { --color-offset: 120ms; }
.wedge6 { --color-offset: 150ms; }
.wedge7 { --color-offset: 180ms; }
.wedge8 { --color-offset: 210ms; }
.wedge9 { --color-offset: 240ms; }
.wedge10 { --color-offset: 270ms; }
.wedge11 { --color-offset: 300ms; }
.wedge12 { --color-offset: 330ms; }

.center-core {
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
  animation: maskMorph 2.4s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.mask-circle {
  animation: maskRadius 2.4s ease-in-out infinite;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
}

@keyframes wedgeRotate {
  from { transform: rotate(0deg); }
  to { transform: rotate(360deg); }
}

@keyframes wedgeSkew {
  0% { transform: rotate(0deg) skewX(0deg); }
  25% { transform: rotate(90deg) skewX(-15deg); }
  50% { transform: rotate(180deg) skewX(0deg); }
  75% { transform: rotate(270deg) skewX(15deg); }
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg) skewX(0deg); }
}

@keyframes wedgeWidth {
  0% { stroke-width: 3; }
  25% { stroke-width: 5; }
  50% { stroke-width: 3; }
  75% { stroke-width: 5; }
  100% { stroke-width: 3; }
}

@keyframes maskMorph {
  0% { r: 30; }
  50% { r: 45; }
  100% { r: 30; }
}

@keyframes maskRadius {
  0% { r: 30; }
  50% { r: 50; }
  100% { r: 30; }
}
</style>

A cascading domino wave where eight rectangular blocks translate downward at staggered velocities (each offset by 60ms), on contact each block skews forward while its border-radius subtly rounds, the stroke-width fattens at the impact contact point, and the shadow beneath stretches horizontally then snaps back creating a rippling topple effect across the sequence.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <defs>
    <filter id="dominoShadow">
      <feGaussianBlur in="SourceAlpha" stdDeviation="2"/>
      <feOffset dx="0" dy="8" result="offsetblur"/>
      <feComponentTransfer>
        <feFuncA type="linear" slope="0.3"/>
      </feComponentTransfer>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode/>
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>
  <g filter="url(#dominoShadow)">
    <!-- Block 1 -->
    <g class="domino-block block1" style="animation-delay: 0ms;">
      <rect class="domino-body" x="25" y="80" width="25" height="80" fill="none" stroke="#FF6B6B" stroke-width="3" rx="2"/>
      <ellipse class="domino-shadow" cx="37.5" cy="175" rx="15" ry="4" fill="#000" opacity="0.2"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Block 2 -->
    <g class="domino-block block2" style="animation-delay: 60ms;">
      <rect class="domino-body" x="62" y="80" width="25" height="80" fill="none" stroke="#4ECDC4" stroke-width="3" rx="2"/>
      <ellipse class="domino-shadow" cx="74.5" cy="175" rx="15" ry="4" fill="#000" opacity="0.2"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Block 3 -->
    <g class="domino-block block3" style="animation-delay: 120ms;">
      <rect class="domino-body" x="99" y="80" width="25" height="80" fill="none" stroke="#45B7D1" stroke-width="3" rx="2"/>
      <ellipse class="domino-shadow" cx="111.5" cy="175" rx="15" ry="4" fill="#000" opacity="0.2"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Block 4 -->
    <g class="domino-block block4" style="animation-delay: 180ms;">
      <rect class="domino-body" x="136" y="80" width="25" height="80" fill="none" stroke="#96CEB4" stroke-width="3" rx="2"/>
      <ellipse class="domino-shadow" cx="148.5" cy="175" rx="15" ry="4" fill="#000" opacity="0.2"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Block 5 -->
    <g class="domino-block block5" style="animation-delay: 240ms;">
      <rect class="domino-body" x="173" y="80" width="25" height="80" fill="none" stroke="#FFEAA7" stroke-width="3" rx="2"/>
      <ellipse class="domino-shadow" cx="185.5" cy="175" rx="15" ry="4" fill="#000" opacity="0.2"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Block 6 -->
    <g class="domino-block block6" style="animation-delay: 300ms;">
      <rect class="domino-body" x="210" y="80" width="25" height="80" fill="none" stroke="#DDA15E" stroke-width="3" rx="2"/>
      <ellipse class="domino-shadow" cx="222.5" cy="175" rx="15" ry="4" fill="#000" opacity="0.2"/>
    </g>
    <!-- Block 7 -->
    <g class="domino-block block7" style="animation-delay: 360ms;">
      <rect class="domino-body" x="247" y="80" width="25" height="80" fill="none" stroke="#BC6C25" stroke-width="3" rx="2"/>
      <ellipse class="domino-shadow" cx="259.5" cy="175" rx="15" ry="4" fill="#000" opacity="0.2"/>
    </g>
  </g>
</svg>
<style>
.domino-block {
  transform-origin: center bottom;
}

.block1 { animation: dominoFall 0.8s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) infinite; }
.block2 { animation: dominoFall 0.8s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) 0.06s infinite; }
.block3 { animation: dominoFall 0.8s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) 0.12s infinite; }
.block4 { animation: dominoFall 0.8s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) 0.18s infinite; }
.block5 { animation: dominoFall 0.8s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) 0.24s infinite; }
.block6 { animation: dominoFall 0.8s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) 0.30s infinite; }
.block7 { animation: dominoFall 0.8s cubic-bezier(0.34, 1.56, 0.64, 1) 0.36s infinite; }

.domino-body {
  animation: blockSkew 0.8s ease-in-out infinite, blockStroke 0.8s ease-in-out infinite, blockRadius 0.8s ease-in-out infinite;
}

.domino-shadow {
  animation: shadowRipple 0.8s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes dominoFall {
  0% { transform: translateY(0) rotateZ(0deg); }
  40% { transform: translateY(0) rotateZ(0deg); }
  70% { transform: translateY(80px) rotateZ(8deg); }
  100% { transform: translateY(0) rotateZ(0deg); }
}

@keyframes blockSkew {
  0% { transform: skewY(0deg); }
  50% { transform: skewY(12deg); }
  100% { transform: skewY(0deg); }
}

@keyframes blockStroke {
  0% { stroke-width: 3; }
  50% { stroke-width: 6; }
  100% { stroke-width: 3; }
}

@keyframes blockRadius {
  0% { rx: 2; ry: 2; }
  50% { rx: 6; ry: 6; }
  100% { rx: 2; ry: 2; }
}

@keyframes shadowRipple {
  0% { rx: 15; ry: 4; }
  50% { rx: 25; ry: 3; }
  100% { rx: 15; ry: 4; }
}
</style>

A radiating sunburst where six geometric rays rotate outward from center while their stroke-width scales rhythmically with rotation angle, stroke-color transitions through yellow-orange-deep-red in a three-phase heating sequence offset by 50ms per ray, a surrounding halo's border-radius morphs from razor-sharp (0%) to completely rounded (50%) and back in sync with ray expansion, and the outer glow's shadow intensifies at peak animation creating concentric ring depth.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <defs>
    <filter id="halo-glow">
      <feGaussianBlur stdDeviation="2" result="coloredBlur"/>
      <feMerge>
        <feMergeNode in="coloredBlur"/>
        <feMergeNode in="SourceGraphic"/>
      </feMerge>
    </filter>
  </defs>
  <circle cx="150" cy="150" r="60" fill="none" class="halo"/>
  <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="50" class="ray ray1"/>
  <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="50" class="ray ray2"/>
  <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="50" class="ray ray3"/>
  <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="50" class="ray ray4"/>
  <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="50" class="ray ray5"/>
  <line x1="150" y1="150" x2="150" y2="50" class="ray ray6"/>
  <circle cx="150" cy="150" r="80" fill="none" class="outer-glow" filter="url(#halo-glow)"/>
</svg>
<style>
svg { display: block; }
.halo {
  stroke: #ffcc00;
  stroke-width: 3;
  animation: halo-morph 3s ease-in-out infinite;
  filter: drop-shadow(0 0 10px rgba(255, 200, 0, 0.6));
}
@keyframes halo-morph {
  0% { border-radius: 0%; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 10px rgba(255, 200, 0, 0.6)); }
  50% { border-radius: 50%; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 20px rgba(255, 100, 0, 0.8)); }
  100% { border-radius: 0%; filter: drop-shadow(0 0 10px rgba(255, 200, 0, 0.6)); }
}
.ray {
  stroke-linecap: round;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
  animation: ray-rotate 3s linear infinite;
}
.ray1 { animation: ray-rotate 3s linear infinite 0ms; stroke: #ffdd00; stroke-width: 4; }
.ray2 { animation: ray-rotate 3s linear infinite 50ms; stroke: #ffaa00; stroke-width: 4; }
.ray3 { animation: ray-rotate 3s linear infinite 100ms; stroke: #ff6600; stroke-width: 4; }
.ray4 { animation: ray-rotate 3s linear infinite 150ms; stroke: #ff3300; stroke-width: 4; }
.ray5 { animation: ray-rotate 3s linear infinite 200ms; stroke: #cc0000; stroke-width: 4; }
.ray6 { animation: ray-rotate 3s linear infinite 250ms; stroke: #990000; stroke-width: 4; }
@keyframes ray-rotate {
  0% { transform: rotate(0deg) scaleY(0.8); stroke-width: 2; }
  50% { transform: rotate(180deg) scaleY(1.2); stroke-width: 6; }
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg) scaleY(0.8); stroke-width: 2; }
}
.outer-glow {
  stroke: rgba(255, 100, 0, 0.5);
  stroke-width: 2;
  animation: glow-pulse 3s ease-in-out infinite;
}
@keyframes glow-pulse {
  0% { filter: drop-shadow(0 0 8px rgba(255, 200, 0, 0.4)); }
  50% { filter: drop-shadow(0 0 30px rgba(255, 100, 0, 1)); }
  100% { filter: drop-shadow(0 0 8px rgba(255, 200, 0, 0.4)); }
}
</style>

A DNA double helix where two intertwined curves rotate 360 degrees while skewing and counter-skewing on alternating axes at staggered 100ms intervals, each strand's stroke-width oscillates inversely (one fattens as the other thins), stroke-dasharray chases in opposite directions at different speeds, color gradients flow through complementary blue-to-orange spectrum offsets, and shadows cast at opposing angles amplify the helical three-dimensional coiling depth.

<svg viewBox="0 0 300 300" width="300" height="300">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="strand1-grad" x1="0%" y1="0%" x2="100%" y2="100%">
      <stop offset="0%" style="stop-color:#0066ff;stop-opacity:1" />
      <stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#ff6600;stop-opacity:1" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="strand2-grad" x1="100%" y1="100%" x2="0%" y2="0%">
      <stop offset="0%" style="stop-color:#ff6600;stop-opacity:1" />
      <stop offset="100%" style="stop-color:#0066ff;stop-opacity:1" />
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="helix-shadow">
      <feGaussianBlur in="SourceGraphic" stdDeviation="1.5"/>
    </filter>
  </defs>
  <path d="M 80,100 Q 150,80 220,100 T 220,200 Q 150,220 80,200" fill="none" stroke="url(#strand1-grad)" stroke-width="8" class="strand strand1" filter="url(#helix-shadow)"/>
  <path d="M 220,100 Q 150,120 80,100 T 80,200 Q 150,180 220,200" fill="none" stroke="url(#strand2-grad)" stroke-width="8" class="strand strand2" filter="url(#helix-shadow)"/>
</svg>
<style>
svg { display: block; }
.strand {
  stroke-linecap: round;
  transform-origin: 150px 150px;
  animation-duration: 3s;
  animation-iteration-count: infinite;
  animation-timing-function: linear;
}
.strand1 {
  animation: helix-spin 3s, strand1-width 3s ease-in-out, stroke-chase1 2s linear;
  stroke-dasharray: 200;
  filter: drop-shadow(5px 5px 10px rgba(0, 100, 200, 0.4));
}
.strand2 {
  animation: helix-spin 3s, strand2-width 3s ease-in-out, stroke-chase2 2.5s linear;

@keyframes helix-spin {

  50% { transform: rotate(180deg) skewY(-20deg); }
  100% { transform: rotate(360deg) skewY(20deg); }
}

  50% { stroke-width: 12; }
  100% { stroke-width: 6; }
}
@keyframes strand2-width {

  50% { stroke-width: 6; }
  100% { stroke-width: 12; }
}

  0% { stroke-dashoffset: 0; }
  100% { stroke-dashoffset: -200; }
}
@keyframes stroke-chase2 {

  100% { stroke-dashoffset: 0; }
}

A falling water droplet that descends via translate then bounces, its clip-path shifts from elongated teardrop to perfect sphere on impact, the droplet's stroke animates as a surface tension line traversing the surface in staggered wave phases (three cycles total), stroke-color fades from saturated blue to pale transparency mid-fall, the shadow beneath stretches horizontally on landing then snaps back, and background-position shifts upward to simulate reflected ripple parallax beneath.

A kaleidoscope pattern where twelve triangular wedges rotate clockwise while skewing on alternating axes in a 360-degree spin, each wedge's stroke-width pulsates to a four-beat rhythm matching the rotation, stroke-color sequences through rainbow gradient with each wedge offset by 30ms, the center clips a rotating mask with morphing border-radius from 0% sharp to 50% rounded, and layered shadows cast at varying opacity (25%, 50%, 75%) suggest radial depth recession.

A bouncing basketball where the ball scales vertically while squashing horizontally on impact (height decreases, width increases), the impact zone clips a radial burst-pattern that expands then contracts, the ball's shadow stretches during flight and compresses on landing in rapid three-phase sequence, a surrounding glow's stroke-width pulses to accentuate each bounce, and opacity of secondary bounces fades in staggered rhythm creating diminishing bounce effect.

A morphing hexagon that cycles through six distinct angular phases while rotating 360 degrees, border-radius on alternating vertices rounds from 0% sharp to 30% bulbous in staggered cycles offset by 100ms, the stroke animates as a color gradient flowing around the perimeter in opposing directions every other phase, and an inner shadow intensifies then lightens at each transformation to suggest dimensional depth shifts and surface curvature.

A spiral that expands outward from center while rotating clockwise continuously, its stroke-dasharray accelerates then decelerates in a breathing four-phase rhythm (fast-slow-fast-medium), stroke-color gradually shifts from cool blue through turquoise to warm orange across a three-second loop, the spiral's intersecting curves morph border-radius from sharp edges to gently rounded (0% to 15%) creating organic fluidity, and a concentric shadow radiates outward then contracts to suggest helical three-dimensional coiling depth.

<svg class="spiral-container" viewBox="0 0 200 200" width="200" height="200">
  <path class="spiral-path" d="M 100 100 Q 110 90 120 100 T 140 120 T 150 150 T 140 180 T 100 190 T 60 180 T 50 150 T 60 120 T 100 110" fill="none" stroke="#0088ff" stroke-width="3"/>
  <circle class="spiral-shadow" cx="100" cy="100" r="80" fill="none" stroke="rgba(0,0,0,0.1)" stroke-width="2"/>
</svg>
<style>
.spiral-container {
  animation: spiralRotate 4s linear infinite;
}

.spiral-path {
  animation: strokeShift 3s ease-in-out infinite, dashBreathe 4s ease-in-out infinite, radiusFlow 3s ease-in-out infinite;
  stroke-dasharray: 200;
  stroke-dashoffset: 0;
  filter: drop-shadow(0 0 15px rgba(0, 136, 255, 0.4));
}

.spiral-shadow {
  animation: shadowRadiate 4s ease-in-out infinite;
}

@keyframes spiralRotate {
  from { transform: rotate(0deg); }
  to { transform: rotate(360deg); }
}

@keyframes strokeShift {
  0% { stroke: #0088ff; }
  50% { stroke: #ff8800; }
  100% { stroke: #0088ff; }
}

@keyframes dashBreathe {
  0% { stroke-dasharray: 300; stroke-dashoffset: 0; }
  25% { stroke-dasharray: 150; stroke-dashoffset: 50; }
  50% { stroke-dasharray: 300; stroke-dashoffset: 0; }
  75% { stroke-dasharray: 200; stroke-dashoffset: 25; }
  100% { stroke-dasharray: 300; stroke-dashoffset: 0; }
}

@keyframes radiusFlow {
  0% { stroke-linecap: butt; }
  50% { stroke-linecap: round; }
  100% { stroke-linecap: butt; }
}

@keyframes shadowRadiate {
  0% { r: 80; opacity: 0.3; }
  50% { r: 120; opacity: 0.05; }
  100% { r: 80; opacity: 0.3; }
}
</style>

A pulsating biological membrane that cycles border-radius from angular (10%) to bulbous (50%) while its stroke-dasharray animates as a chasing wave that accelerates through four phases (slow-80ms-fast-40ms-slow-80ms-medium-60ms), stroke-color shifts from pale pastel cyan to vibrant neon magenta and back in staggered cycles, and the membrane's shadow inverts from cast-outward to cast-inward creating visual expansion-contraction tension.

A geometric tessellation where six hexagons translate inward toward center in staggered 150ms sequence, each hexagon's stroke-width scales as it approaches center (fattening on arrival to 3x original width), stroke-color builds from muted gray-blue to saturated vibrant-blue as shapes converge, border-radius subtly morphs from sharp (0%) to slightly rounded (10%) creating subtle bulging effect, and overlapping shadows darken progressively to suggest layering compression depth.

A confetti cascade where twelve pieces translate downward at staggered speeds (each offset by 80ms), rotating on multiple axes simultaneously, each piece's border-radius morphs from sharp corners (0%) to fully rounded (50%) mid-fall suggesting dimensional tumbling, the shadow beneath stretches vertically during descent then compresses on landing, and opacity fades from fully opaque to transparent creating a cascading, dimensional flutter effect with trailing shadow distortion.

A hinged envelope flap that skews downward from its spine attachment while rotating slightly, the flap's leading edge border-radius rounds from sharp to fully curved (0% to 50%) as the flap closes, the flap's stroke-color shifts from bright white to deep charcoal gray in staggered phases, stroke-width increases with compression (1px to 3px), and the shadow beneath darkens progressively suggesting compressed three-dimensional paper layering and fold depth.

A shearing asterisk where six thin bars rotate while skewing on alternating axes in a staggered 60ms sequence, each bar's stroke-width scales inversely with skew intensity (thin when maximally sheared, thick when straight), stroke-color flashes through a six-phase hue rotation (red-yellow-green-cyan-blue-magenta) offset by 40ms per bar, and shadows beneath each bar shift angle based on its skew orientation creating a six-pointed starburst shadow pattern.
